question.content: Compute the convolution output y(t)=x(t)*h(t) of the two given rectangular signals and write it in expanded unit-step form. Answer format: a fully expanded unit-step expression. Use only the variable names from the problem and ASCII characters; write explicit multiplication signs *; do not use bracket shorthands such as [u(t)-u(t-1)] or compressed grouped forms; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Represent x and h as rectangular pulses in tau-domain and use y(t)=integral x(tau)h(t-tau) dtau.

step_3.content: Flip h to h(-tau), then shift by t to obtain h(t-tau) with support [t-1,t].
step_3.key_point: Overlap interval is intersection of [0,2] and [t-1,t].

step_5.content: Compute overlap length L(t): 0 outside [0,3], ramp up on [0,1], flat on [1,2], ramp down on [2,3].

step_7.content: Output is y(t)=6*L(t), giving trapezoid-like pulse with peak value 6.
step_7.key_point: y(t)=6L(t), yielding a trapezoidal output pulse.
